item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm not too sure on this one. I think a lot of the horror and punch of the article is reliant on reading it close to Easter which is a bit of a weakness. That combined with the 'parasite pretending to be a regular item' trope as well as the rather shaky excuse as to why people would keep eating the egg after opening it (they are out of their heads on a built in drug) push me close to a down vote but not quite all the way. No vote.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) due to being licensed under CC BY-NC-ND 2.0 under this source. As such, it has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good thing that Easter is pretty far as I'm writing this, so I won't remember body horror while eating chocolate…
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Made me remember about Circe, a story written by Julio Cortazar, I've only found the original in spanish, but you may find a translated version somewhere. It's very enjoyable and I sincerely recommend it
Show link
Hide link

[http://www.ciudadseva.com/textos/cuentos/esp/cortazar/circe.htm]

Other than that, this scip was very nice and I liked it, upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for writing this. I love body-horror!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brain kept going, "You said it's male, and that it's egg-laying. What did you mean, exactly, by the word 'male'"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to change a word or two, because biological training is making me twitch. Changing "male" to "anatomically male" to make the twitching stop (and disambiguate).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't people normally chew chocolate? Wouldn't that harm the eggs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goodbye Easter, goodbye frogs, hello upvote.
So, if the eggs were removed, the shell could be melted and reshaped, whereon it could be sold as an actual chocolate? Sounds like a good idea. I want to run the building that sells that chocolate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the Foundation has an unwritten rules against both assuming SCPs are intelligent, and using religious terminology i.e. "Holy Week". I've seen Christmas SCPs that only use December 25, so this just feels odd. Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love holiday SCPs! It's so rare that I get the chance to feel both festive and horrified.

And in this case it adds a bit of flavour that pulls this away from being just "Parasite that looks like a normal thing."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 and ya got me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Note to self: Create a body horror SCP based on Peeps….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lovely, I had severe food poisoning on sunday night/monday morning so now all I can think is that I was throwing up frogs in the dark.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i have problem with that one.
i hate that kind of chocolate anyways. may be natural defense…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank God I haven't eaten any thing chocolate yet…gonna be checking that now. Great idea and I love it! Just creepy enough to make me want to check everything I eat! Definitely upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh fuck, I used to love Lindor truffles. :( Maybe it's a good thing I always bite off the top half before consuming the whole thing.

Really wonderful creep/gross-out article. Boerhaave's syndrome is nasty as hell. I also love how well integrated Easter is into the thing. One suggestion I have is to either spell out Papua New Guinea a second time, or replace "PNG" with "the country" or something similar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: D: Stop scaring me gosh dang it I just got over my "not eating chocolates after reading this" bit!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The inside of your skull must be a terrifying place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, as I love well done anomalous organisms, have an easter +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good god, I nearly choked on the chocolate egg I was eating while reading this.
+1
Think I'll stick to Peeps from now on…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah I don't like parasites and I don't like these kinds of articles as a rule. I'm only upvoting because I love the idea of a child crying as they're vomiting up frogs and being scared of telling mommy and daddy in case they won't let him/her eat candy again but not knowing what to do as they keep vomiting up frogs, sobbing.

EDIT: If my juices will let me I may write a tale about precisely that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really don't like parasites. Especially parasites disguised as normal objects. This is not good enough for me to look past that. Sorry mate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I applaud at your research and timing for this. Take my +1.
Although it doesn't hit me hard enough to cause me to stay away from Easter eggs for a day or two, but that may be because I have eaten strange things before. >.>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liquid Polecat, I believe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm really picky about "weird nature" articles (especially those that are just "X— BUT INSIDE YOU") and I still really enjoyed this one. Maybe the strategically-placed-chocolates twist is what made it work for me. It IS similar to a few other things I've read— and recalls plenty of scifi or anime scenes of zoological vomit, but the squishy aesthetics give this just the right blend of science and squick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Y'know, as the author of that skip, it did indeed slightly tug at my mind that, "Hey, isn't this sorta like your candy bugs…?", but in the end, I really do think that this is different enough and stands on its own merits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote from me, as it's similar to SCP-1517.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…I just finished a box thingy of those Lindt things right before I opened this.
One little thing I'd change is the last line of the containment procedures. 'fed to D-class' seems a bit… Eh. I don't know but it seems a bit off-tone. Maybe cut the bit about feeding to D-class (just say used for research, propagation of SCP-1325, or to be destroyed) and include it later when you talk about propagation.
Also, the description seems to drag on a bit and hops back and forth between stages, if that makes any sense. Could do with a bit of tidying up to streamline more and fix tonal errors.
Is it pyloral valve or pyloral sphincter? Apparently they're the same, might be easier to say pylorus and cover the whole area in one fell swoop - I know how digestion works ok D:

Aside from that it's cool, up voted. Also first feedback, yay

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was a mongoose or ferret, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
DEAR GOD WHY WOULD YOU DO THIS ;~;

I am just sitting here making dismayed faces I hate you I am never eating Lindt truffles again

(Upvoted)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ick. I'll just be over here now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off, I really liked the writing style and how clearly written this is. Well done in those areas.

That said, I have to agree with Scan. It was fairly predictable, falling into the overused tropes of "parasites what grow inside you" and "things what look like inanimate objects". Nothing about it seemed terribly interesting to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: K, removed the last sentence of the containment procedures and footnoted the esophegal rupture statistics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a whole unopened box of chocolate eggs I was planning on eating today, you bastard.

…+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article is decent but theres stuff in it that is just plain unnecessary such as the last sentence of the containment procedures and that entire paragraph droning on about esophegal rupture statistics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well-researched, but I don't feel like this does anything special that other oh-so-clever (in-universe) parasites do just as well, if not better. Plus, spending a paragraph talking about the statistics of esophogeal rupture really detracted from the piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to cut all the chocolate eggs I eat in half beforehand now.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just to clarify, I added that bit after Gaffney brought it up. I did intend to mention it in the first place, but I wasn't sure whereabouts to mention it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On the clones thing

Given that they are clones of the parent, all eggs are genetically identical.

I do agree on the tonal issues though. Felt a bit off, especially during the last paragraph.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What have you done to my favorite food? YOU TURNED IT INTO FROGS THAT'S WHAT D:

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconding what Smapti said. I like the idea, but the tone is pretty shaky at the moment. As a general thing, try to avoid referring to instances of a SCP object as "they/them."

Also, what does "westernized" mean, exactly? You should probably find another way to say it.

Also, also, are the eggs just clones of the father or what?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weirdly interesting. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is better than when I reviewed it in the forum, but the tone still needs improvement. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bleah! (Throws out chocolate eggs.) +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You've obviously done your research. Bravo.

This also reminds me of that animal that is not a cat but has cat in its name and can turn into water, and, when ingested, panics and burrows out of the stomach. What was that one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Happy Easter, everyone! Hope you enjoyed gorging yourselves on chocolate eggs yesterday *smirk*

Oh, and thanks to Smapti for reviewing this.
